nativeRange.habitat: Pelagic freshwater; Amazon and Tocantins river basins, Rio Branco, and Guyana river systems

diet.notes: Accepts frozen meaty foods such as bloodworm, chopped prawn, mussel, and earthworms. Most individuals will take high-quality dried pellets alongside frozen foods. Feed multiple small meals daily.

summary: The Bucktooth Tetra (Exodon paradoxus) is a scale-eating characin from the Amazon basin, best kept as a species-only school of 12 or more. Known for stripping scales and fins from virtually any tankmate, it is not suited to a community aquarium.
careNotes: SPECIES-ONLY TANK REQUIRED. The Bucktooth Tetra is a scale-eater and extreme fin-nipper that should never be housed with any other fish. In groups smaller than about 12, aggression tends to concentrate on the weakest individuals and the group can self-destruct through attrition; larger schools of 25 to 50 individuals have been suggested by some sources as a way to distribute aggression more evenly across the group. Most specimens available in the trade are wild-caught, so acclimatisation to captive foods may take some patience.
breedingNotes: An egg-scatterer that has been bred in captivity, though rarely. A separate, densely planted spawning tank with soft, slightly acidic water is typically used. Eggs and fry are highly vulnerable to being eaten by adults; eggs should be removed promptly after spawning. Fry have been reported to hatch within approximately 3 days.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked scientific name, family/order, native range, temperature, and pH. Adult size listed as 7.5 cm standard length (SL) — excludes caudal fin; total length aligns with the 8–15 cm range across sources.

additional.name: Fishkeeping World
additional.notes: Cross-checked tank size (55 gal / ~208 L for 12 fish), water parameters, diet, lifespan (up to 10 years), and group/compatibility requirements. Aquarium Co-Op (primary for fw-fish slice) returned 404 for this species; SeriouslyFish used as primary, with FishBase and Fishkeeping World as secondaries.

fish.breedingNotes: The Bucktooth Tetra is an egg-scatterer that has rarely been bred in captivity. A separate, dedicated spawning tank is required, and eggs must be removed immediately after spawning to prevent cannibalism by the adults.
